claim: The Agent OS architecture consists of three layers—LPU (model + prompts + prompt chains), I/O (tools + spyware/monitoring), and RAM (state/context)—enabling composable and swappable agent components.
actionable: Structure your agent systems with separate, composable layers (model layer, tool layer, state layer) so you can swap components without rebuilding the entire system.
source_title: Agent OS: LLM OS Micro Architecture

claim: The Language Processing Unit (LPU)—combining model providers, individual models, domain-specific prompts, and prompt chains—is the true differentiator of agent architectures.
actionable: Invest in prompt engineering and prompt chaining strategies as the core innovation layer; model selection matters less than how prompts are orchestrated.
source_title: Agent OS: LLM OS Micro Architecture

claim: Spyware (monitoring, debugging, visualization of agent state) should be a first-class component of agent I/O, not an afterthought, to enable rapid iteration.
actionable: Build logging and monitoring into your agent's I/O layer from the start; log all state mutations, tool calls, and prompt inputs so you can debug failures quickly.
source_title: Agent OS: LLM OS Micro Architecture

claim: You don't need every component of the Agent OS architecture to build a working agent, but proper architecture enables long-term composability and rapid component swapping.
actionable: Use the bare minimum (e.g., single prompt + model + one tool) if it solves your problem, but design with composability in mind for future scaling.
source_title: Agent OS: LLM OS Micro Architecture

claim: Agents should be composable as functions—treating each agent as a black box with typed inputs/outputs—so they can be chained into agentic workflows.
actionable: Design agents with clean input/output schemas so multiple agents can be composed sequentially; ensure output shape from Agent A matches input shape for Agent B.
source_title: Agent OS: LLM OS Micro Architecture

claim: Self-improvement (reward functions that let agents update their own RAM and re-run prompts) is the critical missing piece from both this architecture and the LLM ecosystem.
actionable: When building agents, design for introspection and feedback loops; capture success/failure signals so agents can learn from each execution cycle.
source_title: Agent OS: LLM OS Micro Architecture
